sections.paragraph:
-

(1) to rub, to bruise, to rub out, see מָרֹוחַ . (Cognate are מָרַק, מָרָה, and with ר turned into a sibilant, מָשַׁח. Arab. مَرْخُ a tree from which fire is brought by rubbing.-(2) i.q. Arab. مرخ to rub over, e.g. the body with oil. IV. to soften. In the Old Test. it is once used of a cataplasm laid on a sore, Isa 38:21, “Isaiah had said, let them take dried figs וְיִמְרְחוּ עַל־הַשְּׁחִין (pregn.) and lay them softened upon the ulcer;” LXX. καὶ τρίψον καὶ κατάπλασαι. Hence מָרֹותַ.
